_meta.notes: Wonder events. Each turn rolls a 1% chance to fire one. The selected wonder's narrationFlavor injects into the narrator's prompt for that turn only. Some wonders have an optionalToolEffect that runs through the normal applyTools validation. Per-id cooldown prevents the same wonder from firing twice in a 10-turn window.

wonders.narrationFlavor: A name you don't know is whispered close to your perception. Acknowledge briefly, don't explain.
There is a single eye where there was no eye before. Describe it for one beat. Don't explain it.
A draft passes through that was not there before. The stone remembers it, briefly.
If a roof or sky is visible, the moon is the wrong color tonight. Note it once.
A folded scroll falls from nowhere, landing within reach.
An unseen ledger marks a tally. The room's small luck withdraws a little.
Something deep beneath you breathes once. Don't name it. Don't explain it.
A memory that does not belong to you crosses your awareness — a child's hand, a smell of bread. It passes.
Your shadow grows another shadow for one heartbeat, then resettles.
A small creature you have not seen before is suddenly familiar — as if you had always known its face.
Some hum you didn't know was there has stopped. Now you notice the silence.
A name almost arrives — close enough to taste — and then doesn't.
A letter you didn't write is briefly visible in the dust. Single character. Then gone.
Far away, the rust-hand passes over the world for a heartbeat. You do not see it. You feel it.
Someone is watching. Briefly. The angle is wrong for any room.
Something old weighs on the air. The kind of old that pre-dates the cycle.
A candle that wasn't lit is now lit. It does not warm the air.
A footstep arrives just before its foot. The order is wrong by a beat.
Something that was once a river briefly remembers being a river.
Far above, in some other room of the world, the Choristers are singing. The sound does not reach you, but its absence does.
Old Bone's voice — the prophet's last breath — moves through the walls. Words: not for you.
Cipher or Cinder — one of the Twins — almost speaks. The mouth opens and the moment passes.
There is a taste of salt in the air for no good reason.
Dawn arrives a breath late. The light has the wrong cast.
A pebble in your line of sight rolls one inch backward of its own accord.
The world sighs, audibly, once. Don't dwell on it.
One of your pages turns of its own accord. You did not turn it.
Your core pulses without your prompting. A summon was almost made — by something else.
Something warmer than you presses against your shell for one beat.
Your surface flickers — for a moment you saw a thing you have no eyes for.
